examples: Keep to the track—the land is very boggy around here.
Gley soils are typical of tundra, meadows, and boggy areas.
The team also compared charcoal deposits in boggy meadows within the groves to the tree-ring fire history.
Winter highways are sandy tracks through the boggy landscape, passable only in winter when the ice hardens.
The police have extended their searches, pumped water from boggy ground, brought in special equipment and asked for more specific information.
